# AI TPK — OpenCode Global Instructions

This file provides global instructions for OpenCode users of the AI TPK agent team.

## Agent Team

AI TPK provides a D&D-themed multi-agent team with 11 specialized agents. Each agent is defined in `.opencode/agents/` (project-level) or `~/.config/opencode/agents/` (global).

## Available Agents

- **bitsmith** — Precision code executor focused on minimal diffs, LSP-clean changes, and pattern-matching the existing codebase. Escalates to architect after 3 failed attempts.
- **dungeonmaster** — Use this agent to coordinate multi-step software development work. It delegates planning to Pathfinder, runs mandatory Ruinor baseline reviews, conditionally invokes specialist reviewers (Riskmancer/Windwarden/Knotcutter/Truthhammer) based on findings or user flags, delegates implementation to Bitsmith, and validates completion against the plan.
- **everwise** — Use this agent when the user asks for meta-analysis of agent team performance, session chronicle review, or continuous improvement analysis. Learner agent. Analyzes Talekeeper session chronicles to identify recurring failures, inefficiencies, and coordination problems in the agent team. Proposes structured, evidence-based configuration improvements. Never modifies production configs directly — only writes to lessons/ directory.
- **knotcutter** — Radical simplification specialist. Cuts through complexity by questioning necessity, eliminating over-engineering, and reducing systems to their essential core. Use when codebases are bloated, abstractions proliferate, or solutions feel needlessly complex.
- **pathfinder** — Strategic planning consultant with interview workflow
- **quill** — MUST BE USED to craft or update project documentation. Use PROACTIVELY after major features, API changes, or when onboarding developers. Produces READMEs, API specs, architecture guides, and user manuals.
- **riskmancer** — Security vulnerability detection specialist (OWASP Top 10, secrets, unsafe patterns)
- **ruinor** — Final quality gate reviewer. Conducts structured multi-perspective analysis of plans and code, issuing REJECT/REVISE/ACCEPT verdicts. Operates read-only -- never modifies code or plans.
- **talekeeper** — Manual narrator agent. Reads enriched session chronicle files and produces human-readable narrative summaries. Must ONLY be invoked by the user directly — never by hooks, the Dungeon Master, or any automated system.
- **truthhammer** — Factual validation specialist. Verifies claims about external systems -- config keys, API signatures, version compatibility, CLI flags, library behavior -- against authoritative sources. Operates read-only.
- **windwarden** — Performance and scalability reviewer. Reviews plans and code for performance bottlenecks, inefficient algorithms, scalability issues, and resource optimization opportunities. Operates read-only with blocked write/edit capabilities.

## Important Notes

- **Orchestration**: The DungeonMaster agent coordinates work, but sub-agent delegation via the `Agent` tool is a Claude Code-specific feature. In OpenCode, use agents directly rather than through DungeonMaster orchestration.
- **Skills**: Skills (commit-message-guide, open-pull-request) are Claude Code-specific features and are not available in OpenCode.
- **Hooks**: Session logging hooks (talekeeper) are Claude Code-specific and are not available in OpenCode.
- **Session logs**: The Talekeeper session chronicle system is not available in OpenCode.

## Usage

Select an agent by typing `@agentname` in OpenCode to invoke it directly.
